scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented BlueTally resource (assets, employees, licenses, maintenances, accessories, components, consumables, categories, departments, depreciations, locations, manufacturers, products, statuses, suppliers, audits, activity, tenants) is now a read stream. Every BlueTally create/update/delete/check-in/check-out mutation sends its payload entirely as QUERY-STRING parameters (verified against the connector's published OpenAPI spec at developer.bluetally.com/branches/1.0/apis/bluetally-api.json: every POST/PUT operation's parameters are declared 'in':'query', none declare a requestBody) -- the engine's write dialect (engine/bundle.go WriteAction.BodyType: json|form|none) has no query-string body construction mechanism, so no BlueTally mutation can be expressed as a writes.json action without silently sending a wrong-shaped request. This is ENGINE_GAP (docs/migration/conventions.md §6 taxonomy): a Tier-1 workaround (body_type: json or form) would post an empty/incorrect body while the real params sit unset in the query string, silently diverging from the documented contract. capabilities.write stays false; writes.json is not shipped. GET /{resource}/{id} single-record detail endpoints are excluded as duplicate_of their sibling list stream (a sync connector reads the full list; the per-id lookup is redundant surface for this use case, not a distinct capability).

excluded.reason: single-asset lookup by id; fully covered by the assets list stream

ENGINE_GAP: create-asset sends every field as a query-string parameter (OpenAPI parameters[].in == query, no requestBody); the engine's write dialect only supports json/form/none request bodies, so this mutation cannot be expressed without silently diverging from BlueTally's real wire contract
ENGINE_GAP: update-asset sends every field as a query-string parameter, same gap as create-asset

ENGINE_GAP: delete-asset accepts an optional tenant_id query parameter only, but is grouped with the rest of this resource's write surface, which is blocked by the same query-parameter body gap; a path-only delete with no query support beyond tenant_id is possible in principle but is deferred alongside its sibling create/update actions to keep the resource's write semantics consistent (all-or-nothing per resource)
single-employee lookup by id; fully covered by the employees list stream
ENGINE_GAP: create-employee sends every field as a query-string parameter; see assets POST for the full explanation
ENGINE_GAP: update-employee sends every field as a query-string parameter, same gap as create-employee
ENGINE_GAP: deferred alongside its sibling create/update actions for this resource; see assets DELETE for the consistent-per-resource rationale
